description_long: This object consists of three open-ended wrenches of varying sizes, arranged upright and closely grouped together. Each wrench is made of metal, with visible signs of use such as discoloration and surface wear. The wrenches have hexagonal and U-shaped openings at each end, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The surfaces show a mix of matte and slightly rough finishes, with some areas appearing more polished from handling. One wrench is lighter in color, possibly due to a different alloy or less oxidation, while the others are darker and show more rust. The overall appearance suggests these are well-used hand tools, commonly found in garages or urban maintenance settings.
description: A set of three metal open-ended wrenches, showing signs of wear and rust, grouped together upright. The wrenches vary in size and color, with visible hexagonal and U-shaped jaws.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat faces and open jaws of all three wrenches, with clear size differences and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profiles of the wrenches and the hexagonal openings at the top ends.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the wrenches, with the darker, rusted surfaces more prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profiles and the arrangement of the wrenches, with the lighter wrench in the foreground.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible rust and wear marks on the darker wrenches, especially along the handles and jaws; moderate severity.
